text: Science has a clearer story about coronal mass ejections than social media does. Coronal mass ejections, or CMEs, are massive bursts of solar wind and magnetic fields rising above the solar corona. They can unleash trillions of tons of plasma, affecting satellites, power grids, and even our atmosphere. NASA tracks these events closely, but predicting their exact impact is still a challenge. So, the next time you hear about a CME, remember: it’s not just science fiction; it’s a cosmic reality impacting our tech and lives. Want to dive deeper into the cosmos? Like and follow for more science secrets!
